key_insight: The paper turns mesh-based PINN predictions into certified pointwise error intervals by reconstructing an admissible field on a finite-element mesh and applying deterministic residual-based correction formulas.
engineering_relevance: This gives engineers a way to verify not just averaged loss, but local solution accuracy for PDE-constrained models in applications such as elasticity and reaction-diffusion problems.
method: Build a compatible field by finite-element reconstruction of the PINN prediction.
Compute a certifying residual on the mesh-based finite-dimensional system.
Derive exact or computable pointwise error bounds via adjoint Green representations, with norm-based, localized, randomized, and nonlinear Taylor-remainder variants.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on manufactured examples, a large-scale public 3D elasticity benchmark, and projected neural load families on that benchmark to assess compatibility and calibration.
watch_item: The strongest guarantees depend on mesh compatibility and on how accurately the transfer from discrete to continuous solutions can be bounded, so implementation details matter.
paper_title: Pointwise Error Estimates for Numerical Physics-Informed Neural Networks
